title: A man I sit next to on the bus every day
summary: Describe the person who sits next to you on the bus each morning.
topic: A man I sit next to on the bus every day
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about a person you sit next to on the bus or metro every morning. Say what he looks like, what he does, and what you have noticed. Use present simple.
hintStarters: Every morning, I take the bus number nine in Tashkent and…
An old man sits next to me almost…
sampleText: Every morning, I take the bus number nine in Tashkent. I get on at the third stop. An old man sits next to me almost every day. He always sits by the window. He has short grey hair and a long grey coat. He carries a small leather bag on his knees. He never opens it. He reads the same newspaper every day. He turns the pages very slowly with two fingers. He never looks at his phone. We have travelled together for one year. We do not speak. He does not know my name. I do not know his. When I get on, he nods one small nod. When I get off at my stop, he nods again. Sometimes he gives me his seat by the window. He smells of old soap and a little tobacco. I do not know where he goes. I do not know if he has a family. But the bus does not feel right when his seat is empty. A quiet person can become part of your day without one word.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cùng một tờ báo mỗi ngày

sampleAnnotations.phrase: old soap and a little tobacco
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: mùi xà phòng cũ và một chút thuốc lá

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Adverbs of frequency: 'always', 'never', 'sometimes'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'always', 'never', 'sometimes' đứng trước động từ chính: 'He always sits by the window', 'He never opens it', 'Sometimes he gives me his seat'. Với 'be', đặt sau động từ.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'always', 'never', 'sometimes' go before the main verb: 'He always sits by the window', 'He never opens it', 'Sometimes he gives me his seat'. With 'be', they go after the verb.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Third person singular -s
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Với 'he/she/it', động từ thêm -s hoặc -es: 'sits', 'carries', 'reads', 'turns', 'nods', 'gives'. 'carry' đổi y thành i + es → 'carries'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: With 'he/she/it', add -s or -es: 'sits', 'carries', 'reads', 'turns', 'nods', 'gives'. 'carry' becomes 'carries' (y → i + es).

sampleTranslationVi: Mỗi sáng, tôi đi xe buýt số chín ở Tashkent. Tôi lên xe ở trạm thứ ba. Một ông cụ ngồi cạnh tôi gần như mỗi ngày. Ông luôn ngồi cạnh cửa sổ. Ông có mái tóc bạc ngắn và một áo khoác xám dài. Ông mang một cái túi da nhỏ trên đầu gối. Ông không bao giờ mở nó. Ông đọc cùng một tờ báo mỗi ngày. Ông lật trang rất chậm bằng hai ngón tay. Ông không bao giờ nhìn điện thoại. Chúng tôi đã đi cùng nhau một năm. Chúng tôi không nói chuyện. Ông không biết tên tôi. Tôi không biết tên ông. Khi tôi lên xe, ông gật đầu một cái nhẹ. Khi tôi xuống ở trạm, ông gật đầu lần nữa. Đôi khi ông nhường tôi ghế cạnh cửa sổ. Ông có mùi xà phòng cũ và một chút thuốc lá. Tôi không biết ông đi đâu. Tôi không biết ông có gia đình không. Nhưng xe buýt không cảm thấy đúng khi ghế của ông trống. Một người yên lặng có thể trở thành một phần của ngày bạn mà không cần một lời nào.

mcQuestions.prompt: What bus does the writer take?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I take the bus number nine'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Where does the old man sit?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'He always sits by the window'.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does the old man carry?
options: A small leather bag on his knees
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'He carries a small leather bag on his knees'.
The text says 'He reads the same newspaper every day'.

mcQuestions.prompt: How do they greet each other?
options: He nods one small nod when she gets on and off.
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'When I get on, he nods one small nod. When I get off at my stop, he nods again'.

options: He sits always by the window.
Always he sits by the window.
He always sits by the window.
He sits by always the window.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'always' goes before the main verb: 'He always sits'.
mcQuestions.hint: Adverb of frequency goes before the main verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct third person form: 'He ___ a small leather bag.'
mcQuestions.hint: 'carry' is a -y verb, watch the spelling.

mcQuestions.prompt: How long have they travelled together?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'We have travelled together for one year'.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does the old man smell of?
options: Old soap and a little tobacco
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'He smells of old soap and a little tobacco'.

mcQuestions.prompt: How does the bus feel when his seat is empty?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'the bus does not feel right when his seat is empty'.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why can a person we have never spoken to still become important in our daily life?
